sections.title: What is a quotation?
sections.body: A quotation (or quote) is a formal offer you send a prospective customer stating the price for specific goods or services, usually with a validity period and terms. It is not yet a demand for payment, it is your proposed price. Once the client accepts, the quotation becomes the basis for a sales order or invoice.

sections.title: Quote, win, invoice
sections.body: Paper & Pen keeps the whole sales flow connected so nothing is re-typed and nothing falls through the cracks.
items: Build a quote from saved products and prices, or from scratch
Set a validity date, discounts and your tax rate
Send by WhatsApp or email with an accept-online link
Track status: draft, sent, accepted, declined, expired
Convert an accepted quote into an invoice or sales order in one click

sections.title: Quotation or estimate?
sections.body: A quotation is a fixed, firm price you commit to for a stated period. An estimate is an approximate figure that may change once the work is scoped. Paper & Pen supports both, so you can send a firm quote when you are sure and an estimate when you are still scoping.

faqs.a: Yes. Creating, sending and tracking quotations is free forever on Paper & Pen, with no limit on how many you send.

faqs.q: How does a client accept a quote?
faqs.a: The quote you send carries an accept-online link. The client taps to accept, and you are notified instantly so you can convert it to an invoice.

faqs.q: Can I convert a quote into an invoice?
faqs.a: Yes. One click turns an accepted quotation into an invoice or sales order, carrying over every line, discount and tax so nothing is re-keyed.

faqs.q: Can I set a validity date?
faqs.a: Yes. Add a validity period and the quote shows as expired once it passes, which is a useful nudge for the client to decide.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a quotation and a proforma invoice?
faqs.a: A quotation is a price offer before a deal is agreed. A proforma invoice is a more formal advance bill, often used for deposits, customs or pre-payment, issued once the client intends to buy.

ctaTitle: Send a quote that wins the deal
